meta.title: ArqWellness Lab — Lighting, Energy and Wellness | University of Seville
meta.description: ArqWellness Lab at the University of Seville (IUACC). Research on indoor environmental comfort, circadian lighting, energy efficiency and healthy architecture.

hero.lede: We study how the built environment shapes human health, comfort and energy performance. Over 15 years of teaching, research and knowledge transfer from Seville.

about.title: A laboratory for the architecture of wellbeing
about.lede: ArqWellness Lab — formed by the PAIDI groups TEP-130 and TEP-1000 — brings together professors, researchers and clinicians from Preventive Medicine and Pediatrics around the indoor environment, energy and health in architecture.
about.longA: Our work focuses on the control and management of indoor environments: assessing and improving conditions for comfort, ergonomics and occupant health, from a holistic view of the factors that shape the built habitat.
about.longB: We gather specialists in environmental conditioning and energy efficiency — thermal, acoustic, lighting and air-quality aspects — covering both passive design and active building systems.

mission.title: Mission
mission.text: To advance human wellbeing through healthy, comfortable and sustainable architectural environments.

vision.title: Vision
vision.text: To be an international reference in research on the indoor environment and its impact on health and energy performance.

approach.title: Approach
approach.text: We integrate technical and scientific knowledge with simulation, measurement and design tools to tackle environmental conditioning transversally.

about.imageAlt: ArqWellness Lab researchers in front of the ETSA building

team.title: People behind the lab
team.lede: We combine senior faculty with more than two decades of academic experience with a new generation of researchers trained in architecture, applied physics and preventive medicine.

members.maria: María de la Esperanza de Triana Sánchez Muñoz

research.title: Three lenses on the inhabited space
research.lede: Our research is articulated through three complementary lines that blend empirical work, computational simulation and field studies in real buildings.

comfort.title: Environmental comfort
comfort.summary: Optimising the built environment for human wellbeing and energy efficiency.
comfort.intro: We study the parameters that drive comfort indoors and their interaction with energy consumption.
items: Noise reduction and acoustic comfort strategies
Assessment and improvement of indoor air quality
Post-occupancy evaluations and user satisfaction studies
comfort.outro: We develop guidelines and standards for comfort-driven architectural design.

lighting.title: Lighting & health
lighting.summary: How light shapes the human body beyond visual perception.
lighting.intro: We investigate the influence of daylight and artificial light on physiology, psychology and behaviour.
items: Lighting, circadian rhythms and sleep quality
Impact of daylight on occupant health
Spectral quality of light and biological effects
Biologically effective lighting in real buildings
lighting.outro: We collaborate with health professionals, chronobiologists and manufacturers to bring findings into design.

control.title: Control & integration
control.summary: Smart systems for environmental control in buildings.
control.intro: We design systems that monitor and regulate environmental parameters in inhabited spaces.
items: Smart control of lighting and HVAC
control.outro: We implement and validate these systems in real-world environments.

groups.title: Two research groups, one laboratory
groups.lede: ArqWellness Lab is composed of the PAIDI groups TEP-130 and TEP-1000, both affiliated with the IUACC at the University of Seville.

tep130.name: Architecture, Heritage and Sustainability
tep130.description: Acoustics, lighting, optics and energy. A PAIDI group at the IUACC of the University of Seville and the ArqWellness Lab. It brings together faculty and researchers from Architectural Construction I, Applied Physics I/II/III and Preventive Medicine and Pediatrics.
focus: Design and integration of building services (plumbing, electrical, HVAC, communications, safety)
Indoor environmental conditioning (hygrothermal, acoustic, lighting, IAQ)
Building energy: demand, efficiency and renewables (solar thermal, PV, cogeneration)
Preventive medicine and pediatrics applied to the built environment

tep1000.name: Comfort and Energy Use in Buildings
tep1000.description: A PAIDI group at the IUACC of the University of Seville, hosted at the School of Architecture. It gathers researchers from Architectural Construction I and Preventive Medicine and Pediatrics.
focus: Design and integration of building services and systems
Indoor environmental conditioning: hygrothermal, acoustic, lighting and IAQ
Building energy: demand and efficiency, renewable energies

training.title: Architecture, Health and Zero Energy
training.lede: Elective course of the Bachelor's Degree in Foundations of Architecture at the University of Seville, exploring energy use and the control of comfort, quality and health performance in the built space.

scope.title: Scope
scope.intro: The course addresses, in an integrated way:
items: Control of comfort, quality and health performance

design.title: Energy & healthy design
design.text: Energy efficiency concepts and methods are applied, integrating a healthy, resilient, CO₂-neutral design that is respectful of human health.
design.imageAlt: Indoor Environmental Quality (IEQ) diagram: thermal comfort, air, acoustics and light

content.title: Contents & methodology
items: Assessment and optimisation of energy performance
content.outro: The course stresses efficient energy use and maximising performance for occupants.

practice.title: Simulation & practice
practice.intro: Students apply techniques using simulation and analysis tools, organised in teams of 3 or 4.
practice.imageAlt: Energy simulation model of the Seville School of Architecture in BIM software
practice.outro: Includes interactive activities, analysis sessions, continuous assessment and a final exam based on design-improvement exercises.

projects.title: Applied research & knowledge transfer
projects.lede: Representative selection of competitive R&D projects and transfer contracts developed by the lab's groups.
projects.disclaimer: Representative selection. Full catalogue available on SISIUS — University of Seville.

project1.title: Energy efficiency in heritage buildings
project1.description: Research on compatible strategies to improve energy efficiency in heritage buildings while preserving their historical value.
outcomes: Development of non-invasive energy retrofitting strategies

project2.title: Circadian lighting in healthcare facilities
project2.description: Implementation and monitoring of circadian lighting systems in healthcare environments to improve patient recovery and staff performance.
outcomes: Custom lighting control algorithms for patient rooms
Before/after comparative study of staff performance

project3.title: Adaptive comfort standards for Mediterranean climate
project3.description: Development of climate-specific comfort standards for Mediterranean conditions applicable to sustainable building design.
outcomes: Climate-specific comfort parameters for Mediterranean buildings
Seasonal variation model for adaptive comfort
Design recommendations for passive cooling strategies

project4.title: Lighting assessment for cultural heritage
project4.description: Assessment and implementation of lighting systems for cultural heritage buildings, focusing on conservation and visitor experience.
outcomes: Assessment methodology for cultural heritage lighting

project5.title: Energy monitoring on a university campus
project5.description: Design and implementation of an integrated monitoring system for energy consumption across campus buildings.

publications.title: Indexed journal articles
publications.lede: A sample of the lab's recent scientific output in JCR journals on energy efficiency, lighting and comfort.
publications.disclaimer: Representative selection. Full catalogue available on SISIUS — University of Seville.

pub1.title: Daylighting design strategies for energy saving in historic buildings
pub1.abstract: Research on daylighting strategies to improve energy efficiency in heritage buildings while preserving their historic value. Combines field measurements, simulation analyses and case studies.

pub2.title: Circadian lighting effects on hospital staff performance: A field study
pub2.abstract: Field study examining the effects of circadian lighting on healthcare staff performance — alertness, cognitive function and subjective wellbeing — under different lighting conditions.

pub3.title: Thermal comfort assessment in Mediterranean university buildings
pub3.abstract: Comprehensive thermal comfort assessment in Mediterranean university buildings, analysing seasonal variations and occupant preferences.

pub4.title: Energy retrofitting strategies for historical buildings in Seville
pub4.abstract: Analysis of energy retrofitting strategies for historical buildings in Seville, evaluating intervention effectiveness and cost-benefit ratios.

pub5.title: Visual comfort and energy efficiency in educational buildings
pub5.abstract: Examines the relationship between visual comfort and energy consumption in educational facilities through measurement and simulation.

contact.title: Let's talk about your project
contact.lede: For collaborations, transfer contracts, internships or academic queries, the ArqWellness Lab team is based at the School of Architecture in Seville.
